description: IEEE 33-Bus fault and reconfiguration scenario: 40-step quasi-static simulation anchored at 2017-06-07 18:00 UTC (evening peak). Three-phase event: 13 pre_fault snapshots (nominal) → 7 faulted_isolated snapshots (Section-8 fault, buses 8-17 dead zone) → 20 restored snapshots (tie-line 34 closed, topology reconfigured). Visual proof of voltage collapse and recovery. Supports System 2 state-estimator stress-testing.

panels.description: Bus voltage magnitudes (pu) for ALL 33 buses across the 40-step event, drawn as lines. The dead-zone buses (8-17) collapse to 0 pu during the faulted_isolated block (steps 13-19) and recover on tie-line restoration. Healthy buses remain near 0.99 pu throughout. Threshold markers at 0.95 and 1.05 pu.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "fault_event")
  |> range(start: 2017-06-07T17:59:00Z, stop: 2017-06-07T18:03:00Z)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "bus")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field == "vm_pu")
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"])

panels.title: Bus Voltage Profile (pu)

panels.description: OLTC feeder transformer tap position (integer steps, -5 to +5). During the faulted_isolated block, the OLTC reference bus 17 is inside the dead zone, so the tap is pinned at the evening-peak value (-2 BOOST) with run_control=False. On restoration, OLTC control resumes and the tap remains at -2 as the restored vmin is already 0.9971 pu.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "fault_event")
  |> range(start: 2017-06-07T17:59:00Z, stop: 2017-06-07T18:03:00Z)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "system")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field == "tap_pos")
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"])

panels.title: OLTC Tap Position (integer steps, -5 to +5)

panels.description: Total served load (MW) and slack/substation feed-in (MW) across all 40 steps. Drop visible in the faulted_isolated block (~2.69 MW served vs ~3.29 MW in pre_fault) when buses 8-17 are isolated. Full recovery in the restored block as tie-line 34 re-energises the dead zone.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "fault_event")
  |> range(start: 2017-06-07T17:59:00Z, stop: 2017-06-07T18:03:00Z)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "system")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field == "total_load_mw" or r._field == "slack_p_mw")
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"])

panels.title: Served Load & Slack Feed-in (MW)

panels.description: Dead-bus count from the event measurement's n_dead_buses field: 0 in pre_fault, 10 during faulted_isolated (buses 8-17), 0 again in restored. The phase tag labels each block. This is the SPEC-8 three-phase visual marker — a 0→N→0 pulse aligned exactly to the three event blocks.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "fault_event")
  |> range(start: 2017-06-07T17:59:00Z, stop: 2017-06-07T18:03:00Z)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "event")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field == "n_dead_buses")
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"])

panels.title: Event Phase / Dead-bus Pulse (phase-tag marker)

panels.description: Bus voltage magnitudes (pu) for the dead-zone buses only (bus_id 8-17 = article buses 9-18), drawn as lines. These buses collapse to 0 pu when isolated (steps 13-19, faulted_isolated block) and recover to ~0.997 pu on tie-line restoration (restored block, steps 20-39). min=0 is intentional to make the collapse explicit.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "fault_event")
  |> range(start: 2017-06-07T17:59:00Z, stop: 2017-06-07T18:03:00Z)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "bus")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field == "vm_pu")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.bus_id == "8" or r.bus_id == "9" or r.bus_id == "10" or r.bus_id == "11" or r.bus_id == "12" or r.bus_id == "13" or r.bus_id == "14" or r.bus_id == "15" or r.bus_id == "16" or r.bus_id == "17")
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"])

panels.title: Dead-Zone Bus Voltage (buses 8-17) — collapse to 0 → recovery

panels.description: Net active power (MW) at every bus from res_bus.p_mw (pandapower sign convention: positive = net consumption / load draw at the bus, negative = net injection). The dead-zone buses (8-17) drop to 0 MW during the faulted_isolated block and return on restoration — the per-bus load-shed-and-restore story that complements the voltage view. Slack bus 0 reads ~0.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "fault_event")
  |> range(start: 2017-06-07T17:59:00Z, stop: 2017-06-07T18:03:00Z)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "bus")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field == "p_mw")
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"])

panels.title: Bus Active Power (MW) — per-bus load shed & restore

panels.description: Minimum bus voltage (pu) across energised buses only in the restored block (steps 20-39, 2017-06-07T18:01:00Z to 18:03:00Z). The energised==1 filter excludes zero-fills so dead buses during earlier phases do not corrupt the stat. Expected restored min ~0.9971 pu (clean, above 0.95 green threshold). Colour thresholds: green >= 0.95, orange >= 0.94, red below 0.94.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "fault_event")
  |> range(start: 2017-06-07T18:01:00Z, stop: 2017-06-07T18:03:00Z)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "bus")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field == "vm_pu")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.energised == "1")
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"])

panels.title: Restored Block — Min Bus Voltage (pu)

panels.description: Per-snapshot topology and event state from the event measurement. Columns: _time, faulted_line_id (7), tie_closed (0/1), tie_id (-1 if open, 34 when restored), n_dead_buses (0→10→0), dead_buses (csv of bus indices). The phase tag distinguishes the three blocks. This table is the ground-truth topology log for System 2 state-estimator integration.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "fault_event")
  |> range(start: 2017-06-07T17:59:00Z, stop: 2017-06-07T18:03:00Z)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "event")
  |> pivot(rowKey: ["_time"], columnKey: ["_field"], valueColumn: "_value")
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"])

panels.title: Topology / Event State per Snapshot

title: IEEE 33-Bus — Fault & Reconfiguration
